How deep down the rabbit hole do you want to go in terms of improving site optimization and the custom experience? I've been doing this since 2005 and this is a deep field in its own right and would probably be deserving of its own training program. There's so much depth that you can go into of tweaking and optimizing but for most people the analysis that we'll cover here is enough so understanding people's pain points and doing some data-based analysis doing some tracking of what how users are interacting with your site will give you lots of clues as to how to improve the conversion rate on your landing page and through your funnel to give you better performance in Facebook but the custom experience framework that I've developed over this period goes deeper into looking at many different parts of this so like when I start off with analysis I'm looking for the pain points, the clues, the data points that I can kind of build into my testing so the reporting in Insights is many different ways that you can do this. Analytics is the most obvious so in Facebook you have the data on your site most likely you'll have some analytics installed so if you don't have anything installed at the very least install something like Google Analytics which is free so that you can start to see some of the data inside your ad account and see what's working and what the opportunities are within there as well then kind of going a bit further into things like surveys so as you start to generate leads as you start to generate sales and you get traffic to your site you want to ask more direct questions so in this section we talk about online polls and asking people if there's anything that's stopping them from buying or signing up today you can extend that to things like net promoter score and asking customers after they've purchased your product or service and received it whether they'd recommend other people as well we use it ourselves for for our training programs for our e-com stores because it's a great gauge as to whether you're on the right track and it's partly are you selling your product or service to the right people and are they experiencing the outcome that you promised them so it's a big part of building trust, building your brand and building your kind of lifetime value as well things like customer service, satisfaction scores as well so any of the interactions that your customers may have pre and post purchase you have to track those and you have to see whether your customer service team are representing your product and service in the best light as well then we go into things like session capture so you can install tools which track the mouse usage on the page how people are scrolling so you can create heat maps where they're clicking etc building of personas so we talk about I like to look at is not just getting caught up in my ad campaigns and thinking about different opportunities to target people it's also expanding out into what's happening around us so if for example we're heading into a key holiday period like Christmas or Halloween or Easter or summer holidays is there a pivot or an angle that my product can have there so if i'm selling ecom or if i'm selling an info product can i hook the message onto something that is around or maybe it's a sporting event or some kind of major political event campaign planning plays a big part into kind of how you build your funnels your landing page etc creative testing goes without saying you need to be constantly trying new angles and ideas merchandising so if you're running an ecom store there are many errors of merchandising that you need to consider so for example what products in your categories are your top sellers what is your stock availability like so do you have enough stock to sell this product and you know for example if product is really selling well but you don't have enough stock then maybe you need to take it away from the kind of front end and surface something else and start testing different products behavioral targeting is an awesome way of really really improving that custom experience by seeing their behavior coming into the site and then personalizing it and offering them a architecture on your site so you know one thing i i talk about when it comes to the types of user coming on your site there's the hunters there's the trackers and explorers and and your site has to tailor for these three types of people because for any number of visitors coming in only a small percentage are going to be buying right now so you know your site conversion rate could be 2% 4% 6% whatever they are the people that have landed on your site are now ready to buy then you'll have another 20 30% of people who are in the kind of research and consideration phase but they're not ready to buy and then you've got a bigger number that are just not going to buy no matter what you do and that's okay but understanding that and then tailoring your experience around that makes a huge difference in funneling those people who are maybe that 60 or 70 that clicked into your site not ready to buy now maybe not even next week or two weeks time but you can build up that brand familiarity you can build up that interaction with them so that even when they're ready then you can target them with the ads at the right time and get them into your funnel based on your type of product and your kind of consideration path for purchase as well and then you've got other things like additional functionality and landing pages designs products promotions so for example you may have a lead gen experience and increase my conversion rate by speaking their language so someone who's spending a hundred dollars a day on their ads and hasn't really made that much profit or revenue is in a
